title: Capability Contract
description: A typed, governed contract that defines a Capability as the primary abstraction for agentic program execution. Replaces SKILL.md as the primary unit — Skill is absorbed as one implementation profile (implementations[].type: "skill"). Routing is Intent → Capability → Execution Engine, with implementation selection controlled by the embedded selector field.

id.description: Dotted hierarchical capability identifier. Example: engineering.quality.code-review

version.description: Semver version of this capability contract

description.description: Natural language description used for semantic matching by ORCHID O-0

signature.description: Type-level contract: what goes in, what comes out, what must be true before and after

type.description: Semantic type name, used for structural matching

schema.description: Relative path or URI to JSON Schema for validation

inputs.description: Named input parameters with their types

type.description: Semantic type name of the output

schema.description: Relative path or URI to JSON Schema for validation

outputs.description: Named output results with their types

preconditions.description: Conditions that must be true before this capability can execute

postconditions.description: Conditions guaranteed true after successful execution

effects.description: Declared side effects. Convention: may_read_*, may_write_*, may_delete_*, may_restart_*, may_modify_*

idempotency.description: Idempotency classification. Examples: idempotent, idempotent_with_freshness(5m), non_idempotent

compensation.description: Compensating capability ID or action for undo

permissions.description: Permission and resource constraints for execution

scope.description: Permission scopes as boolean flags. Convention: resource:action. Example: {"repo:read": true, "repo:write:comments": true, "cluster:deploy": false}

governance.description: Governance metadata: risk classification, approval policy, ownership

risk.description: Risk classification used by Archon Guard for capability token issuance decisions

owner.description: Team or individual responsible for this capability's lifecycle

review_interval.description: ISO 8601 duration for periodic governance review. Example: P90D, P6M, P1Y

freshness.description: Freshness windows for capability validity. Maps to Harmovela envelope valid_from/valid_until/stale_after semantics.

valid_from.description: Timestamp when this capability version becomes effective

valid_until.description: Timestamp when this capability version expires

stale_after.description: ISO 8601 duration after which the capability is considered stale. Example: P180D, P6M

enterprise.description: Enterprise architecture classification. Optional — not all capabilities need business mapping.

regions.description: Geographic regions where this capability is applicable

selector.description: Implementation selection logic embedded in the capability contract itself — NOT a separate routing layer. Resolves which implementations[] entry to use based on execution context.

strategy.description: Selection strategy: priority (first matching rule wins), context_match (best context fit), first_available (first reachable runtime)

when.description: Context conditions that trigger this preference. Common keys: sandbox (docker|wasm|none), model (cloud|local), network (enabled|disabled), tenant

rules.description: Ordered preference rules. First matching rule determines the implementation.

default.description: Fallback implementation type when no rules match

type.description: Implementation profile: skill (LLM prompt in SKILL.md), script (deterministic code), workflow (defined process), mcp (model context protocol server), api (REST/gRPC), service (long-running service)

path.description: Relative file path (for skill/script/workflow), MCP server name, or API base URL

entrypoint.description: Optional entrypoint function or command for scripts and workflows

runtime.description: Target runtime environment for this implementation

description.description: Brief description of this specific implementation

implementations.description: Concrete implementations of this capability. Skill is one profile among many — routing layer never sees this distinction.

conformance.description: Capability conformance verification metadata. Mirrors Harmovela's C0-C3 conformance model extended with CAP-C4 governance.

level.description: Certified conformance level. CAP-C0=schema valid, CAP-C1=golden cases, CAP-C2=adversarial, CAP-C3=composition invariants, CAP-C4=governance integrity

golden_cases.description: Paths to golden test fixture files (NDJSON format, one input event per line)

adversarial_cases.description: Paths to adversarial test fixture files (malformed inputs, boundary conditions, attack payloads)

benchmark.description: Benchmark identifier or path for performance and accuracy measurement

last_verified.description: Timestamp of the last successful conformance verification

verified_by.description: Actor or system that performed the last verification
